preamble: The shift from the alveolar fricative [s] to the labiodental fricative [f] is a change in the place of articulation. It is relatively rare as a systematic sound law but occurs sporadically due to acoustic similarity—both are high-frequency voiceless fricatives that can be misperceived in rapid or noisy speech.

examples.note: Sporadic historical variation between sibilant and labiodental endings in early English dialects.
While starting from a dental fricative, Russian's systemic replacement of front fricatives with [f] demonstrates the acoustic and articulatory proximity used in substitution.
